DOC_78EF7645FCEB.title: cbt001
DOC_78EF7645FCEB.summary: central board of direct taxes ministry of finance, government of india circular no.: cbdt/circular/04/2026 f.no. 275/09/2026-it(b) date: march 25, 2026 subject: revised tds rates and threshold limits for fy 2026-27 — clarification on section 194c, 194j, 194h, and 194i with applicability to technology service contracts, professional fee payments, and rent transactions to: all p ministry of finance, government of india circular no.: cbdt/circular/04/2026 f.no. 275/09/2026-it(b) date: march 25, 2026 subject: revised tds rates and threshold limits for fy 2026-27 — clarification on section 194c, 194j, 194h, and 194i with applicability to technology service contracts, professional fee payments, and rent transactions to: all principal chief commissioners of income tax all chief commissioners of income tax all principal commissioners of income tax all assessing officers all tax deductors and collectors (tan holders) --- ackground the finance act 2026 has amended the tds provisions under chapter xvii-b of the income tax act, 1961. this circular provides consolidated guidance on the revised tds rates, enhanced threshold limits, and new compliance obligations effective april 1, 2026 (assessment year 2027-28). all tax deductors holding a valid tan are required to note these changes and update their deduction logic before the first payment of fy 2026-27. --- ection 194j — professional and technical services the tds rate under section 194j is revised as follows: a) fees for professional services (doctors, lawyers, architects, accountants): rate revised from 10% to 10% (no change) threshold: enhanced from rs. 30,000 to rs. 50,000 per annum b) fees for technical services (it, software, engineering consultancy): rate revised from 2% to 2% (no change) threshold: enhanced from rs. 30,000 to rs. 50,000 per annum c) royalty payments (software licenses, ip usage fees): rate: 10% threshold: rs. 50,000 per annum (enhanced from rs. 30,000) key clarification: payments to it and software comp

DOC_EBA6B7BE810D.title: RBI FEMA
DOC_EBA6B7BE810D.summary: reserve bank of india foreign exchange management act, 1999 rbi/2025-26/147 a.p. (dir series) circular no. 28 date: march 20, 2026 subject: revised timelines and reporting requirements for export of goods and software — softex form submission, export realisation, and letter of undertaking (lut) renewal for fy 2026-27 to: all authorised dealer category-i banks all exporters of foreign exchange management act, 1999 rbi/2025-26/147 a.p. (dir series) circular no. 28 date: march 20, 2026 subject: revised timelines and reporting requirements for export of goods and software — softex form submission, export realisation, and letter of undertaking (lut) renewal for fy 2026-27 to: all authorised dealer category-i banks all exporters of goods and software all sez units and stpi-registered software exporters --- ackground the reserve bank of india, in exercise of powers conferred under section 10(4) and section 11(2) of the foreign exchange management act, 1999 (fema), and all powers enabling it in this behalf, has reviewed the existing export compliance framework. pursuant to representations received from industry bodies including fieo, nasscom, and cii regarding operational difficulties, this circular revises certain timelines and introduces new digital reporting requirements effective april 1, 2026. all exporters of goods and software holding an import export code (iec) are required to note these changes and update their compliance processes accordingly. --- urrent requirement under the existing framework, exporters of software, it services, and it-enabled services are required to submit softex forms to the designated software technology parks of india (stpi) or special economic zone (sez) authorities within 30 days of invoice generation. evised requirement (effective april 1, 2026) the submission timeline for softex forms is revised as follows: a) for invoices up to usd 25,000 (or equivalent): softex submission within 30 days of invoice date (no change) b) for invoices abov

(DIR Series) Circular No. 28 Date: March 20, 2026 Subject: Revised Timelines and Reporting Requirements for Export of Goods and Software — SOFTEX Form Submission, Export Realisation, and Letter of Undertaking (LUT) Renewal for FY 2026-27 To: All Authorised Dealer Category-I Banks All Exporters of

DOC_F19745B1A0EC.title: GST IMS
DOC_F19745B1A0EC.summary: goods and services tax council secretariat government of india gst advisory no.: gstn/advisory/2026/08 date: march 18, 2026 subject: mandatory implementation of invoice management system (ims) for all gst filers — operational guidelines, reconciliation requirements, and penalty framework effective april 1, 2026 to: all gst registered taxpayers all tax deductors and collectors gst advisory no.: gstn/advisory/2026/08 date: march 18, 2026 subject: mandatory implementation of invoice management system (ims) for all gst filers — operational guidelines, reconciliation requirements, and penalty framework effective april 1, 2026 to: all gst registered taxpayers all tax deductors and collectors under gst all gst practitioners and chartered accountants all authorised service providers on gstn --- ntroduction the goods and services tax network (gstn) has developed the invoice management system (ims) to address the persistent problem of input tax credit (itc) mismatches between supplier filings (gstr-1) and recipient claims (gstr-3b). the ims creates a real-time invoice-level reconciliation dashboard on the gst portal that enables recipients to accept, reject, or keep invoices in pending status before itc is claimed. effective april 1, 2026, use of ims is mandatory for all gst-registered taxpayers with aggregate annual turnover exceeding rs. 5 crore in fy 2025-26. voluntary use is available for all other registered taxpayers from the same date. --- upplier action when a supplier saves or files their gstr-1 or gstr-1a, all b2b invoices are automatically pushed to the ims dashboard of the corresponding recipient. the recipient sees these invoices in real time before filing their gstr-3b. ecipient actions available for each invoice appearing in ims, the recipient can take one of three actions: a) accept — confirms the invoice is correct and itc will be claimed in gstr-3b b) reject — marks the invoice as incorrect; supplier must issue a credit note c) pending — defers the decision

ingest.first_chunk_preview: GOODS AND SERVICES TAX COUNCIL SECRETARIAT GOVERNMENT OF INDIA GST Advisory No.: GSTN/Advisory/2026/08 Date: March 18, 2026 Subject: Mandatory Implementation of Invoice Management System (IMS) for All GST Filers — Operational Guidelines, Reconciliation Requirements, and Penalty Framework Effective April 1, 2026 To: All GST Registered Taxpayers All Tax Deductors and Collectors
